reasoning: France are the clear market favorites, having moved to roughly +135/+150 after reaching a third straight semifinal, and they've been utterly dominant, outscoring opponents 16-2 without conceding in any knockout match while Mbappé (8 goals) and Dembélé (5) tear it up. Spain are arguably the tournament's best pure footballing side (outscoring rivals 11-1) but must beat France in the semifinal, and if they advance the draw makes them a strong runner-up bet. On the other half of the bracket, Argentina's defending-champion pedigree and Messi/Martínez experience are real, but back-to-back nervy comeback wins over Egypt and 10-man Switzerland exposed vulnerabilities. England, surging after a gutsy 3-2 win at the Azteca and Bellingham's knockout-stage rampage, are a live outsider and slight favorite over Argentina in their semi. The main threats to France are Spain's control in the semifinal and a rejuvenated England should the bracket break their way.
